perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Confidentiality Agreement, an "affiliated Receiving Party" mean s and includes a parent, if any, of Receiving Party and all present and future companies in whic h Receiving Party, or its parent, individually or collectively, directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, owns or controls fifty percent (50%) or more of the outstanding stock having the right to vote for or appoint directors thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Confidentiality Agreement, an "affiliated Receiving Party" means and includes any entity that has a business relationship with the Receiving Party, regardless of ownership. This includes, but is not limited to, subsidiaries, partners, and contractors.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a conflict by redefining "affiliated Receiving Party." The original definition focuses on ownership (50% or more), while the modified definition broadens it to any business relationship, regardless of ownership. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty regarding which entities are bound by the 'affiliated Receiving Party' obligations, particularly concerning disclosure of proprietary information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Proprietary Information shall be held in stri ct confidence by Receiving Party and shall not be used by Receiving Party except for (i) the pur poses described in this Agreement, unless disclosure or further use is authorized or consented to in writing by Disclosing Party which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or dela yed and/or (ii) the case where disclosure of the same is required under applicable law or by a gove rnmental order, rule or regulation or by the regulations of any relevant stock exchange or other governmental authority (provided that the Receiving party shall give written notice of such re quired disclosure to the other party prior to the disclosure). Such consent to disclose Proprietary Information to employees of Receiving Party or its affiliated company (“affiliated Receiving Party”) with a legitimate “need to know” and only for the purposes described in this Agreement is herewith given, but further consent shall be required for disclosure to others or authorization of use by others. For purposes of this Confidentiality Agreement, an "affiliated Receiving Party" means and includes any entity that has a business relationship with the Receiving Party, regardless of ownership. This includes, but is not limited to, subsidiaries, partners, and contractors. Prior to any use or disclosure of Propr ietary Information by or to another, Receiving Party shall ensure that the re cipient has entered into an agreement limiting the recipient's disclosure and use of Proprietary Informa tion consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "affiliated Receiving Party" from a definition based on ownership to one based on any business relationship. The contradicted text relies on the definition of "affiliated Receiving Party" to determine who can receive Proprietary Information with automatic consent. The change broadens this definition significantly, creating a contradiction because it's no longer clear who qualifies as an "affiliated Receiving Party" under the agreement, impacting the disclosure rules. The contradiction is created by the inconsistency in the definition, making it unclear which entities are subject to the proprietary information restrictions. This adheres to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall remain in full force and effect from the effective date indicated above. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving the other Party sixty (60) days’ written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall remain in full force and effect from the effective date indicated above. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving the other Party thirty (30) days’ written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by altering the termination notice period from sixty (60) days to thirty (30) days. This creates uncertainty about the correct notice period required for termination, potentially leading to disputes over breach of contract if one party relies on the original sixty-day period while the other acts on the basis of thirty days.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall remain in full force and effect from the effective date indicated above. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving the other Party thirty (30) days’ written notice. However the confidentiality obligations and all powers, rights and duties provided herein shall extend for a period of five (5) years following the termination of this Agreement. Neither Party shall assign this Ag reement to any third party, without the prior written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a sixty (60) day written notice for termination. The changed text alters this to thirty (30) days. The contradicted text specifies a thirty (30) day written notice for termination. The change from sixty (60) to thirty (30) in the changed text creates a contradiction because the contract now contains two different notice periods for termination, as the original text before the change (sixty days) contradicted the contradicted text (thirty days). Therefore there is a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: However the confidentiality obligations and all powers, rights and duties provided herein shall extend for a period of five (5) years following the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: However the confidentiality obligations and all powers, rights and duties provided herein shall extend for a period of one (1) year following the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by altering the duration of confidentiality obligations post-termination, from five (5) years to one (1) year. This directly contradicts the original term and causes uncertainty on how long those obligations extend, post termination of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall remain in full force and effect from the effective date indicated above. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving the other Party thirty (30) days’ written notice. However the confidentiality obligations and all powers, rights and duties provided herein shall extend for a period of one (1) year following the termination of this Agreement. Neither Party shall assign this Ag reement to any third party, without the prior written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the confidentiality obligations extend for five years, while the changed text reduces this period to one year. The contradicted text explicitly states the confidentiality obligations extend for a period of one year following termination. Therefore, the changed text initially aligns with the contradicted text. However, considering the perturbation explanation, the original 5-year period directly conflicts with the now contradicted 1-year period (and contradicted text) causing a strong contradiction and uncertainty regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations, and follows the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of Delaware, without regard to conflicts of law principles.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of New York, without regard to conflicts of law principles.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by changing the governing law from Delaware to New York. This fundamental alteration introduces uncertainty about which state's laws will apply to the interpretation and enforcement of the agreement, potentially leading to legal disputes about which jurisdiction controls.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of New York, without regard to conflicts of law principles. Any dispute, controversy and/or difference which may arise between the Parties out of or in relation to or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach thereof, which cannot be settled by amicable mutual accord without undue delay, shall be filed in the courts of Delaware.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement is governed by the laws of Delaware. The changed text modifies this to New York. The contradicted text explicitly states that the agreement is governed by the laws of New York, but disputes are filed in Delaware courts. The change in the governing law from Delaware in the original text to New York in the changed text creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted text uses both New York and Delaware, implying an inconsistency that is not resolved and thus meets the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute, controversy and/or difference which may arise between the Parties out of or in relation to or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach thereof, which cannot be settled by amicable mutual accord without undue delay, shall be filed in the courts of Delaware.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute, controversy and/or difference which may arise between the Parties out of or in relation to or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach thereof, which cannot be settled by amicable mutual accord without undue delay, shall be resolved through binding arbitration in New York City.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the dispute resolution mechanism. Originally, disputes were to be filed in the courts of Delaware. Now, they are to be resolved through binding arbitration in New York City. This creates conflict regarding where and how disputes will be resolved.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of New York, without regard to conflicts of law principles. Any dispute, controversy and/or difference which may arise between the Parties out of or in relation to or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach thereof, which cannot be settled by amicable mutual accord without undue delay, shall be resolved through binding arbitration in New York City.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the original forum for dispute resolution (Delaware courts) with binding arbitration in New York City. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which also specifies binding arbitration in New York City. Both texts occupy the same space in the contract, leading to an internal contradiction regarding where disputes will be resolved. This fits the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition where different sections of a contract provide conflicting resolution, which in this case it creates conflict regarding which terms should be enforced.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that this Agreement is severable and that in the event any provision of this Agreement is held to be ille gal, invalid or enforceab le, the legality, validity and enforceability of the remaining provisi ons will not be affected or impaired.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that this Agreement is severable. In the event that any provision of this Agreement is held to be illegal, invalid or unenforceable, the entire agreement shall be deemed null and void.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the severability clause. The original clause states that if one provision is invalid, the rest of the agreement remains in effect. The modified clause states that if any provision is invalid, the entire agreement becomes null and void. This creates uncertainty about the agreement's overall enforceability if any part is challenged.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreemen t of the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes and can cels all prior communications, understandings and agreements between the Parties hereto relating to the Proprietary Information, whether written or oral, expressed or implied. The Parties agree that this Agreement is severable. In the event that any provision of this Agreement is held to be illegal, invalid or unenforceable, the entire agreement shall be deemed null and void. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have respectively caused duplicates of this document to be executed on their behalf by their proper offi cers and employees thereunto duly authorized.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction to the contradicted text. The original text's severability clause ensured the agreement's continuation even if one provision was invalid, which is also stated in the contradicted text. The changed text alters this, stating that the entire agreement is void if any provision is invalid. This directly contradicts the severability clause that appears in the contradicted text, creating a clear inconsistency regarding the agreement's enforceability.
